plugins.name: luly-v0-4-1
plugins.description: Author Luly flows end-to-end from a single natural-language prompt. v0.4.1 (patch): course landing now has a 'Back' button (→ hub) next to 'Learn' — previously there was no way back to the hub from a course. Applied to authored + template courses. v0.4.0 (minor — template courses): academies can now have multiple courses, where extra ones are content-less 'template' stubs (title + description card + a default 3-lesson scaffold the user fills later). Opt-in only — default is one authored course; add stubs by asking for several/placeholder courses (luly-plan `## Template courses`). Stubs are built from a sanitized copy of the app's default-course template with backgrounds + control colors stripped (product theme applies everywhere) and the shared branded placeholder for card/icon. v0.3.3 (patch — logos & image sizes): real-logo bug fix — loadInlineSvg now strips a leading <?xml?>/DOCTYPE/comments so downloaded brand logos (Wikipedia/CDN) actually reach the header + hub (were silently dropped); header prefers the icon-only mark, hub prefers the lockup-with-name; logos are real-or-asked (never fabricated — ask the user when none found; dead hub-logo.svg removed); card covers are abstract (no text/wordmark/product); image sizes aligned to the app — square 800×800 media placeholder (1:1 block default), wide ~10:3 card cover (1600×480) matching the app default card.png, icon 1:1. v0.3.2 (patch): fix a v0.3.1 regression — the validator required body.theme.layout, but the assembler now omits it for default flows, so every flow without layout overrides failed validation. Validator now treats layout as optional (shape-checked only when present), matching assembler + renderer. v0.3.1 (minor): layout-token fix — flow layout overrides use the renderer's maxWidthDesktop/paddingDesktop (+ *Mobile) keys; the dead bare maxWidth default is dropped (flows keep the app default 1480px width). Color application — luly-style documents quiz answer-card token roles (onSurface/primaryLight/successLight/failureLight) and dark-callout secondary, with contrast targets for legible answer text. Per-viewport awareness — *Mobile style tokens and per-block margins documented as agentic-edit polishing levers, not emitted in simple generation.
